articles.title: DOJ Drops Probe Into Fed Chair Powell; Court Blocks Asylum Ban
articles.summary: The Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over alleged lies about building renovations. This clears the way for Kevin Warsh to be confirmed as his successor. Meanwhile, a federal appeals court ruled President Trump's executive order banning asylum at the southern border is illegal. The FDA also announced it will fast-track three psychedelic drugs for mental health treatment. A tornado in Oklahoma destroyed dozens of homes but caused no fatalities.

articles.title: Pittsburgh: From Steel City to Sports Mecca Hosting the NFL Draft
articles.summary: Pittsburgh is hosting the NFL draft for the first time in nearly 80 years, with up to 700,000 fans expected. The city's 16 major pro championships, including the Steelers' four Super Bowls in the 1970s, make it a natural fit. Locals credit the blue-collar work ethic from the steel industry. The draft, held at Acrisure Stadium, runs through Saturday.

articles.title: Petro Makes Historic Visit to Venezuela After Maduro's US Abduction
articles.summary: Colombian President Gustavo Petro became the first foreign leader to visit Venezuela since the US military abducted Nicolas Maduro in January. He met interim President Delcy Rodriguez in Caracas to discuss border security, trade, and migration. Petro has criticized the US operation as an "assault on sovereignty." Rodriguez has cooperated with some US demands while seeking to attract investors and lift sanctions.
